claim: Inositol lipid 3-phosphatase PTEN converts Ptdlns(3,4)P 2 into phosphatidylinositol 4-phosphate.

result.claim: Inositol lipid 3-phosphatase PTEN converts Ptdlns(3,4)P 2 into phosphatidylinositol 4-phosphate.
result.reasoning: The claim states that Inositol lipid 3-phosphatase PTEN converts PtdIns(3,4)P2 into phosphatidylinositol 4-phosphate. Evidence from paper 24294572, "PTEN Regulates PI(3,4)P2 Signaling Downstream of Class I PI3K", directly supports this. The abstract of this paper explicitly states, "Here we show that PTEN also functions as a PI(3,4)P2 3-phosphatase, both in vitro and in vivo." A 3-phosphatase removes the phosphate group at the D3 position of the inositol ring. Therefore, if PTEN acts as a 3-phosphatase on PtdIns(3,4)P2 (phosphatidylinositol 3,4-bisphosphate), the removal of the 3-phosphate group would result in phosphatidylinositol 4-phosphate (PtdIns(4)P). While the paper does not explicitly name "phosphatidylinositol 4-phosphate" as the product in the extracted propositions, the described enzymatic activity of PTEN on PtdIns(3,4)P2 directly leads to this product. The paper's focus on PTEN's role as a PI(3,4)P2 3-phosphatase provides strong mechanistic support for the claim.

evidence.text: PTEN also functions as a PI(3,4)P2 3-phosphatase both in vitro and in vivo.
evidence.source: PTEN Regulates PI(3,4)P2 Signaling Downstream of Class I PI3K

evidence.text: PTENP1 is a pseudogene of the PTEN tumor suppressor gene.

evidence.text: PTENP1 can regulate cellular levels of PTEN.
